name: Justice Democrats
subject: Not left vs. right, but bottom against the top
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Recently, Justice Democrats’ Communications Director Usamah Andrabi sat
down for an interview with WBUR (Boston’s NPR station) to discuss the best
way for Democrats to respond to Donald Trump in his second term.

As Usamah said, Democrats right now need to be an opposition party.

That’s not left versus right. It’s uniting the bottom against the top —
that’s what voters are desperate to see from their leaders and their
elected officials.

No Democrat in Congress should be voting for a Trump cabinet nominee while
Trump is outright trying to rob this country blind of its Social Security
and Medicaid to raise taxes for everyday people and cut taxes for the
wealthy. But multiple did.

If we don’t have power in Congress at this moment, as Republicans hold the
majority in both chambers, then we need to show people what we are
actually going to fight for. Show us what an agenda for working-class and
everyday people looks like, then dare Republicans to vote against that.

We have to be honest with ourselves about that. The Democratic Party lost
more voters who make less than $100,000 a year this past election cycle
and gained voters who make over $100,000 a year. That is not a party that
is fighting for the working class.

Justice Democrats is actively recruiting working-class candidates to
represent working-class people in Congress. We’re going toe-to-toe with
out-of-touch Republicans and Democrats in the 2026 midterm elections, and
our success depends directly on support from everyday folks like you — not
corporate PACs.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250426?refcode=em20250426-36619&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36619_1203867_iWCkJX&akid=36619%2E1203867%2EiWCkJX ]With our April end-of-month fundraising deadline coming up this
Wednesday at midnight, can we count on you to chip in $5 or
more right now to ensure we reach our goal?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250426?refcode=em20250426-36619&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36619_1203867_iWCkJX&akid=36619%2E1203867%2EiWCkJX ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250426-36619-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36619_1203867_iWCkJX&akid=36619%2E1203867%2EiWCkJX

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36619%2E1203867%2EiWCkJX

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).

subject: Denton today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
Beto just held a town Hall in Denton, Texas this afternoon:
This is how we will fight for our Democracy while Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress get more extreme by the day.
We have to come together to fight voter suppression in Texas. Powered by People and Beto are committed to building community and deep organizing.
Beto is hoping to keep hitting the road connecting with Texans where they are, listening to people's concerns, and organizing to build a brighter future together.
[**Will you help Powered by People host more town halls soon? Every donation will help us host more events across Texas.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250426-2.ncUl1J&refcode=ema20250426-240087&t=1&akid=240087%2E444211%2E1pv736)

More soon,
